const String weapon_name(int weapon_type.brief: Retrieves the name of a weapon based on its type.
const String weapon_name(int weapon_type.details: This function searches through a predefined table of weapons to find a matching weapon type. If a match is found, it returns the corresponding weapon name. If no match is found, it returns the default string "exotic".

params.weapon_type: An integer representing the type of the weapon to look up.

const String weapon_name(int weapon_type.returns: A String object containing the name of the weapon if found, or 'exotic' if the weapon type is not in the table.
const String weapon_name(int weapon_type.name: weapon_name

const String extra_bit_name(const Flags.brief: Generates a string of names for set bit flags from extra_flags.
const String extra_bit_name(const Flags.details:
Returns the names of set bit flags from the extra flags vector.
The function extra_bit_name takes a Flags object as input and uses it to determine which bit flags are currently set in the extra_flags vector. It then calls the print_bit_names function to generate a String object containing the names of these set flags. If no flags are set, the function returns a String with the word 'none'. This function is useful for obtaining a human-readable representation of the active configuration options represented by the flags.

params.flags: A Flags object used to determine which bit flags are currently set.

const String extra_bit_name(const Flags.returns: A String object containing the names of all set flags from the extra_flags vector, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String extra_bit_name(const Flags.name: extra_bit_name

const String wiz_bit_name(const Flags &f.brief: Generates a string of names for set bit flags in the Flags object.
const String wiz_bit_name(const Flags &f.details:
Generates a string listing the names of set bit flags.
The function iterates over a predefined table of bit flags and their corresponding names, checking each flag to see if it is set in the provided Flags object. If a flag is set, its name is appended to a buffer string, separated by spaces. The function returns a string containing the names of all set flags, or 'none' if no flags are set.

params.flags: A reference to a Flags object containing the set of bit flags to be checked.

const String wiz_bit_name(const Flags &f.returns: A String object containing the names of the set bit flags, separated by spaces. Returns 'none' if no flags are set.
const String wiz_bit_name(const Flags &f.name: wiz_bit_name

const String wear_bit_name(const Flags &.brief: Retrieves the names of set wear bit flags.
const String wear_bit_name(const Flags &.details: This function calls the print_bit_names function with a predefined set of wear flags and the provided Flags object. It returns a String containing the names of all wear-related bit flags that are currently set in the Flags object. If no wear flags are set, it returns a String with the word 'none'.

params.flags: A Flags object used to check which wear bit flags are currently set.

const String wear_bit_name(const Flags &.returns: A String object containing the names of all set wear flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String wear_bit_name(const Flags &.name: wear_bit_name

const String act_bit_name(const Flags &f.brief: Generates a string representation of active bit flags for a player or NPC.
const String act_bit_name(const Flags &f.details: The function act_bit_name constructs a string that lists the names of active bit flags for either a player or a non-player character (NPC). It uses the print_bit_names function to generate the list of active flags. If the npc parameter is true, the function prefixes the result with 'npc ' and uses the act_flags set; otherwise, it prefixes with 'player ' and uses the plr_flags set. This function is useful for debugging or displaying the current state of flags in a human-readable format.

params.flags: A Flags object that contains the current state of bit flags to be evaluated.
params.npc: A boolean indicating whether the flags belong to a non-player character (true) or a player (false).

const String act_bit_name(const Flags &f.returns: A String object containing the names of all active flags, prefixed by 'npc ' or 'player ', or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String act_bit_name(const Flags &f.name: act_bit_name

const String room_bit_name(const Flags &.brief: Retrieves the names of set room bit flags.
const String room_bit_name(const Flags &.details: This function takes a Flags object representing a set of room-related bit flags and returns a String containing the names of all the flags that are currently set. It utilizes the print_bit_names function to generate this list based on a predefined flag table, room_flags. If no flags are set, the function returns a String with the word 'none'.

params.flags: A Flags object that holds the current state of bit flags to be checked against the room_flags.

const String room_bit_name(const Flags &.returns: A String object containing the names of all set room flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String room_bit_name(const Flags &.name: room_bit_name

const String plr_bit_name(const Flags &f.brief: Retrieves the names of set player bit flags.
const String plr_bit_name(const Flags &f.details:
Returns the ASCII names of set player flags.
The function plr_bit_name takes a Flags object representing a set of player-related bit flags and returns a String containing the names of all flags that are currently set. It utilizes the print_bit_names function, passing a predefined flag table (pcdata_flags) and the provided Flags object to generate the output. This function is useful for obtaining a human-readable representation of the active player flags.

params.flags: A Flags object that contains the current state of player-related bit flags to be checked.

const String plr_bit_name(const Flags &f.returns: A String object containing the names of all set player flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String plr_bit_name(const Flags &f.name: plr_bit_name

const String off_bit_name(const Flags &f.brief: Generates a string listing the names of bit flags that are set to off.
const String off_bit_name(const Flags &f.details: This function utilizes the print_bit_names function to produce a string that contains the names of all bit flags that are currently set to off in the provided Flags object. It uses a predefined set of flags, off_flags, to determine which flags are considered. The resulting string lists the names of these flags, separated by spaces, or returns 'none' if no flags are set to off.

params.flags: A Flags object used to check which bit flags are currently set to off.

const String off_bit_name(const Flags &f.returns: A String object containing the names of all flags that are set to off, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set to off.
const String off_bit_name(const Flags &f.name: off_bit_name

const String form_bit_name(const Flags &.brief: Generates a string of names for the set bit flags.
const String form_bit_name(const Flags &.details: This function utilizes the print_bit_names function to create a string that lists the names of all bit flags currently set in the provided Flags object. It uses a predefined flag table, form_flags, to determine the names associated with each bit. If no flags are set, the function returns the string 'none'.

params.flags: A Flags object that contains the current state of bit flags to be checked.

const String form_bit_name(const Flags &.returns: A String object containing the names of all set flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String form_bit_name(const Flags &.name: form_bit_name

const String part_bit_name(const Flags &.brief: Generates a string of names for set bit flags.
const String part_bit_name(const Flags &.details:
Generates a string of names for the set bit flags in a Flags object.
The function part_bit_name takes a Flags object and returns a String containing the names of the bit flags that are currently set. It utilizes the print_bit_names function, passing a predefined set of flag configurations (part_flags) and the provided Flags object to determine which flags are set. The resulting string lists the names of these flags, separated by spaces, or returns 'none' if no flags are set.

params.flags: A Flags object used to determine which bit flags are currently set.

const String part_bit_name(const Flags &.returns: A String object containing the names of all set flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String part_bit_name(const Flags &.name: part_bit_name

const String comm_bit_name(const Flags &.brief: Retrieves the names of communication-related bit flags that are set.
const String comm_bit_name(const Flags &.details:
Retrieves the names of communication bit flags that are set.
This function takes a Flags object representing a set of bit flags and returns a String containing the names of all communication-related flags that are currently set. It utilizes the print_bit_names function to generate this list based on a predefined set of communication flags. If no flags are set, the function returns a String with the word 'none'.

params.flags: A Flags object representing the current state of bit flags to be checked against the communication flag table.

const String comm_bit_name(const Flags &.returns: A String object containing the names of all set communication flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String comm_bit_name(const Flags &.name: comm_bit_name

const String revoke_bit_name(const Flags.brief: Generates a string of names for revoked bit flags.
const String revoke_bit_name(const Flags.details: This function calls the print_bit_names function with a predefined set of revoke_flags and the provided Flags object. It returns a String containing the names of all bit flags that are set within the revoke_flags context. If no flags are set, it returns 'none'. This function is useful for identifying which specific revoke-related flags are active in a given Flags object.

params.flags: A Flags object used to determine which bit flags are currently set.

const String revoke_bit_name(const Flags.returns: A String object containing the names of all revoked flags that are set, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no such flags are set.
const String revoke_bit_name(const Flags.name: revoke_bit_name

const String cgroup_bit_name(const Flags.brief: Retrieves the names of set bit flags from a Flags object.
const String cgroup_bit_name(const Flags.details: The function cgroup_bit_name takes a Flags object as input and uses the print_bit_names function to generate a string that lists the names of all bit flags that are currently set in the provided Flags object. It utilizes a predefined vector of flag_type objects, cgroup_flags, which contains the mapping of flag names to their respective bit positions. This function is useful for obtaining a human-readable representation of the active flags in a Flags object.

params.flags: A Flags object that contains the bit flags to be checked.

const String cgroup_bit_name(const Flags.returns: A String object containing the names of all set flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String cgroup_bit_name(const Flags.name: cgroup_bit_name

const String censor_bit_name(const Flags.brief: Generates a string of names for set bit flags related to censorship.
const String censor_bit_name(const Flags.details: This function utilizes the print_bit_names function to produce a string that lists the names of bit flags that are currently set in the provided Flags object, specifically for flags related to censorship. It uses a predefined set of flag definitions, censor_flags, to determine which flags are relevant. The resulting string will contain the names of all set flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.

params.flags: A Flags object that contains the current state of bit flags to be checked against the censor_flags configuration.

const String censor_bit_name(const Flags.returns: A String object containing the names of all set censorship-related flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no such flags are set.
const String censor_bit_name(const Flags.name: censor_bit_name

const String cont_bit_name(const Flags &.brief: Generates a string of names for set bit flags from a Flags object.
const String cont_bit_name(const Flags &.details:
Generates a string listing the names of set bit flags from a predefined set.
The function cont_bit_name takes a Flags object as input and utilizes the print_bit_names function to produce a String containing the names of all bit flags that are currently set within the provided Flags object. The names are derived from a predefined vector of flag_type objects, cont_flags, which associates each bit flag with a human-readable name. If no flags are set, the function returns a String with the word 'none'.

params.flags: A Flags object used to check which bit flags are currently set.

const String cont_bit_name(const Flags &.returns: A String object containing the names of all set flags, separated by spaces, or 'none' if no flags are set.
const String cont_bit_name(const Flags &.name: cont_bit_name

const String item_name(int item_type).brief: Returns the name associated with a given item type.
const String item_name(int item_type).details: This function takes an integer representing an item type and searches the internal item_table for a matching entry. If a matching entry is found, it returns the corresponding name as a String object. If no match is found, it returns the String "none". The returned String can be used for display or identification purposes within the application.

params.item_type: An integer representing the type of item whose name is to be retrieved.

const String item_name(int item_type).returns: A String object containing the name associated with the specified item type, or "none" if the type is not found.
const String item_name(int item_type).name: item_name

const String item_type_name(Object *obj).brief: Returns a string representing the item type name based on the object's item_type attribute.
const String item_type_name(Object *obj).details: This function takes a pointer to an Object and examines its item_type attribute. It maps the item_type value to a corresponding descriptive String. If the item_type is recognized, it returns the associated String; otherwise, it logs a bug message indicating an unknown type and returns '(unknown)'. This function is useful for obtaining human-readable item type names for display or logging purposes.

params.obj: Pointer to the Object whose item_type attribute is to be converted into a descriptive String.

const String item_type_name(Object *obj).returns: A String object containing the name of the item type corresponding to the object's item_type attribute, or '(unknown)' if the type is unrecognized.
const String item_type_name(Object *obj).name: item_type_name

const String affect_loc_name(int locatio.brief: Returns a string description of a given affect location code.
const String affect_loc_name(int locatio.details: This function takes an integer representing an affect location code and returns a corresponding String object with a human-readable description. It covers various predefined affect location constants, providing descriptive labels for each. If the location code is unrecognized, it logs a bug message and returns '(unknown)'. The function is used to translate internal affect location identifiers into user-friendly strings for display or logging purposes.

params.location: An integer code representing the affect location, used to determine the descriptive string.

const String affect_loc_name(int locatio.returns: A String object containing the descriptive name of the affect location corresponding to the input code.
const String affect_loc_name(int locatio.name: affect_loc_name

const String dam_type_name(int type).brief: Returns the string representation of a damage type based on its integer code.
const String dam_type_name(int type).details: This function maps an integer damage type code to its corresponding descriptive string. It covers predefined damage types such as 'fire', 'cold', 'acid', etc. If the provided type code does not match any known damage type, the function logs a bug message and returns '(unknown)'. The function is used to obtain a human-readable name for damage types in the system.

params.type: An integer representing the damage type code to be converted into a string.

const String dam_type_name(int type).returns: A String object containing the name of the damage type corresponding to the input code, or '(unknown)' if the code is unrecognized.
const String dam_type_name(int type).name: dam_type_name

const String get_color_name(int color, i.brief: Retrieves the color name corresponding to the specified color code and style.
const String get_color_name(int color, i.details: This function searches a predefined color table for an entry matching the given color code and bold style flag. If a matching entry is found, it returns the associated color name as a String object. If no match is found, it returns an empty String. The function provides a way to obtain human-readable color names based on numeric color identifiers and style attributes, facilitating color-related display or processing tasks.

params.color: The integer code representing the color to look up.
params.bold: An integer flag indicating whether the color style is bold (non-zero) or not (zero).

const String get_color_name(int color, i.returns: A String object containing the name of the color if found; otherwise, an empty String.
const String get_color_name(int color, i.name: get_color_name

const String get_color_code(int color, i.brief: Retrieves the color code string corresponding to the specified color and boldness settings.
const String get_color_code(int color, i.details: This function searches a predefined color table for an entry matching the provided color and bold parameters. If a matching entry is found, it returns the associated color code as a String object. If no match is found, it returns an empty String. The function allows clients to obtain the appropriate color code based on visual styling preferences, facilitating consistent color representation in output or UI components.

params.color: An integer representing the desired color value to look up in the color table.
params.bold: An integer indicating whether the color should be displayed in bold style; typically 0 for false, non-zero for true.

const String get_color_code(int color, i.returns: A String object containing the color code corresponding to the specified color and boldness, or an empty String if no matching entry exists.
const String get_color_code(int color, i.name: get_color_code

const String get_custom_color_name(Chara.brief: Retrieves the custom color name associated with a character's color setting in a specific slot.
const String get_custom_color_name(Chara.details: This function determines the color name for a given character and slot by checking if the character is a player character (not an NPC) and has a custom color set for that slot. If so, it uses the character's specific color and boldness settings; otherwise, it defaults to the global csetting_table values for that slot. The function returns the color name as a String, which can be used for display purposes.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object whose color setting is to be retrieved.
params.slot: Integer index representing the specific color slot to query.

const String get_custom_color_name(Chara.returns: A String containing the name of the color corresponding to the specified character's color and boldness settings for the given slot.
const String get_custom_color_name(Chara.name: get_custom_color_name

const String get_custom_color_code(Chara.brief: Retrieves a custom color code for a character based on their settings or default configuration.
const String get_custom_color_code(Chara.details: This function returns a color code as a String for a specified character and color slot. If the character is a player character (not an NPC) and has a custom color setting for the slot, it uses that setting; otherwise, it defaults to the global color setting from csetting_table. The function combines the color and boldness attributes to generate the appropriate color code via get_color_code.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object for which the color code is to be retrieved.
params.slot: Integer index representing the specific color slot to be used.

const String get_custom_color_code(Chara.returns: A String containing the color code corresponding to the character's custom or default color settings for the specified slot.
const String get_custom_color_code(Chara.name: get_custom_color_code
